parameters.content:
### Daily Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — daily at 6:00. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Keep Valid Tickets

### Keep Valid Tickets

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Detect Sentiment

### Detect Sentiment

Scores the text's sentiment; ports fire positive / neutral / negative.

Ports: ①→ Escalate Negative — Gmail · ②→ Log Neutral Feedback, Capture Failure Context · ③→ Thank the Sender

⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### Anthropic Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Detect Sentiment** as `languageModel`

🔑 Anthropic API key

### Escalate Negative — Gmail

Sends an email via Gmail — subject is the first line of the message body, recipient comes from the item's `email` field.

Ports: ①→ Collect All Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Log Neutral Feedback

Creates a new page in the Notion database — the title is pulled straight from the item's key field.

Ports: ①→ Collect All Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Thank the Sender

Posts a message to Slack (`#support`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Ports: ①→ Collect All Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Collect All Outcomes

Rejoins 3 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Stamp Processing Time

### Stamp Processing Time

Stamps `processedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Sort Tickets Newest First

### Sort Tickets Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Tag Pipeline Metadata

### Tag Pipeline Metadata

Shapes the item: writes `pipeline`, `version`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Guard Non-Empty subject

### Guard Non-Empty subject

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Notion)

### Append Audit Row (Notion)

Creates a new page in the Notion database — the title is pulled straight from the item's key field.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Gmail · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Send Summary to Gmail

Sends an email via Gmail — subject is the first line of the message body, recipient comes from the item's `email` field.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Gmail

### Escalate Failure to Gmail

Sends an email via Gmail — subject is the first line of the message body, recipient comes from the item's `email` field.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2

# Analyze customer feedback with Notion and Trello

**Pattern:** Survey & Feedback · **Level:** Advanced · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Analyze customer feedback with Notion and Trello. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual feedback is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Daily Schedule → Keep Valid Tickets → Detect Sentiment → Escalate Negative — Gmail → Collect All Outcomes → Stamp Processing Time → Sort Tickets Newest First → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to survey & feedback without manual effort
- Anyone automating feedback in customer support
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Saves hours of manual work each week
- Scales without extra headcount

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Anthropic API key; Google OAuth2; Notion integration token; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

A three-way branch on sentiment, not a single IF, because 'neutral' deserves different handling than 'negative' — quietly logging positive feedback while paging a human only for genuine complaints keeps the alert channel's signal-to-noise ratio high for whoever is on the other end.

## Practice exercises

You've got the basics covered — push further:

1. Add a logging step to the neutral branch too and compare its volume against the negative branch.
2. Test borderline phrases through the sentiment node to see where the positive/neutral line actually falls.
3. Add a 4th branch for a specific emotion if your sentiment provider supports finer-grained labels.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your feedback process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
